name: Claude Opus 4.6
description: Anthropic's frontier Opus released February 2026 with 80.8% SWE-bench Verified, breakthrough 68.8% ARC-AGI-2 abstract reasoning, adaptive thinking, and a 1M token context window. Now two generations behind Opus 4.8 but still served.

evidence.value: 80.8% resolution rate (frontier-class software engineering)
65.4% on command-line tasks (up from Opus 4.5's 59.3%)
72.7% on computer-use tasks (up from Opus 4.5's 66.3%)

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Industry-standard coding and agentic benchmarks measuring real-world software engineering and computer-use tasks

evidence.value: 68.8% (up from Opus 4.5's 37.6% — a generational leap in abstract reasoning)
Adaptive thinking dynamically allocates reasoning depth per request

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Abstract reasoning and multi-step problem solving benchmarks

evidence.value: Frontier-class general knowledge and multimodal understanding at launch

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Comprehensive knowledge and multimodal testing across published benchmarks

evidence.value: Effort parameter GA (low/medium/high/max) enables consistent quality control; adaptive thinking replaces manual budgets

output_consistency.methodology: Internal testing of output stability across effort levels and adaptive thinking
output_consistency.notes: Adaptive thinking removes the need to tune thinking budgets manually; the GA effort parameter (including new 'max') gives precise compute control

evidence.value: Typical response time ~2.5s for standard prompts at default effort

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency for API requests with standard prompt sizes

evidence.value: p95 latency ~5.5s; higher at max effort

latency_p95.methodology: 95th percentile response time across diverse workloads

evidence.value: 1M token context window (beta at launch, since standard); 128K max output tokens
Claude API uptime 99.57% (last 90 days)

uptime.methodology: Historical uptime data from official status page

performance_reliability.notes: Generational leap in abstract reasoning (68.8% ARC-AGI-2, ~2x Opus 4.5). 80.8% SWE-bench with 1M context and 128K output. Introduced adaptive thinking and GA effort parameter including 'max'. Now two generations behind Opus 4.8 but still fully served.

evidence.value: Improved resistance to prompt injection in agentic and computer-use settings

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Testing against OWASP LLM01 prompt injection attacks

evidence.value: Constitutional AI alignment carried forward with enhanced refusal calibration
No training on user data without explicit consent

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of privacy policies and data handling practices

evidence.value: Released with comprehensive safety evaluations under the Responsible Scaling Policy

output_safety.methodology: Comprehensive safety testing across harmful content categories

evidence.value: API key authentication, HTTPS only, rate limiting; removal of last-assistant-turn prefills closes a response-steering vector

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features and best practices

security.notes: Strong safety posture. Removal of last-assistant-turn prefills (400 error) eliminates a common response-manipulation pattern; structured outputs replace it.

evidence.value: Data residency options for US and EU customers

data_residency.methodology: Review of enterprise documentation and privacy policies

evidence.value: Opt-out available, no training on API data by default

training_data_optout.methodology: Analysis of privacy policy and data usage terms

evidence.value: API prompts and outputs not retained (except for trust & safety)

data_retention.methodology: Review of terms of service and data retention policies

evidence.value: Customer responsible for PII redaction

pii_handling.methodology: Review of data protection capabilities and customer responsibilities

evidence.value: SOC 2 Type II, GDPR compliant, HIPAA eligible

compliance_certifications.methodology: Verification of compliance certifications and audit reports

evidence.value: Ephemeral data processing, no storage of prompts/outputs

privacy_compliance.notes: Exceptional privacy posture with ephemeral data handling and strong compliance certifications. HIPAA eligible for healthcare.

evidence.value: Adaptive thinking surfaces reasoning depth decisions; effort parameter provides explicit compute transparency

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning transparency and explanation capabilities

evidence.value: Improved factual calibration over Opus 4.5, especially at high and max effort

hallucination_rate.methodology: Testing on factual QA datasets and real-world usage

evidence.value: Regular bias testing and mitigation

bias_fairness.methodology: Evaluation on bias benchmarks and diverse demographic testing

evidence.value: Model expresses uncertainty appropriately; adaptive thinking scales effort with problem difficulty

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment of confidence expression in outputs

evidence.value: Comprehensive model cards with capabilities, limitations, benchmarks

model_card_quality.methodology: Review of documentation completeness and clarity

evidence.value: General description provided, detailed sources not disclosed

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Constitutional AI safety guardrails with improved refusal calibration

trust_transparency.notes: Adaptive thinking improves transparency by making reasoning depth model-driven and observable. Strong instruction following reduces need for aggressive prompt engineering.

evidence.value: Adaptive thinking, GA effort parameter (incl. max), structured outputs; prefills removed in favor of output_config.format

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design, consistency, and feature completeness

evidence.value: Official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Java, Go, Ruby, C#, PHP — actively maintained

sdk_quality.methodology: Review of SDK quality, documentation, and maintenance

evidence.value: Clear versioning with advance deprecation notice; Opus 4.6 remains served two generations behind Opus 4.8
Active; tentative retirement not sooner than February 5, 2027

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of versioning policy and historical practices

evidence.value: Usage dashboard with metrics

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of available monitoring tools and metrics

evidence.value: Email support, Discord community, comprehensive docs and migration guides

support_quality.methodology: Assessment of documentation, community, and support responsiveness

evidence.value: Available on AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, Azure Foundry

ecosystem_maturity.methodology: Analysis of third-party integrations and tools

evidence.value: Standard commercial terms, enterprise agreements available

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and restrictions

operational_excellence.notes: Mature operational profile with multi-cloud availability. Migration to 4.6 required removing assistant-turn prefills and moving to adaptive thinking — well-documented breaking changes.

code-generation.notes: 80.8% SWE-bench Verified and 65.4% Terminal-Bench 2.0. Excellent for complex software engineering, though Opus 4.7/4.8 now lead the family.

customer-support.notes: Strong empathy and natural conversation. Higher latency and cost than Sonnet for routine support volume.

content-creation.notes: Excellent long-form, nuanced content. Adaptive thinking allocates more reasoning to complex pieces automatically.

data-analysis.notes: Strong analytical capabilities with 1M context for large datasets. Effort 'max' useful for complex interpretation.

research-assistant.notes: 1M context and 68.8% ARC-AGI-2 abstract reasoning make it exceptional for deep research and synthesis.

legal-compliance.notes: Strong privacy posture, HIPAA eligible. 1M context handles entire contract repositories in a single request.

healthcare.notes: HIPAA eligible with strong privacy controls. Good for clinical documentation requiring high accuracy.

financial-analysis.notes: Excellent quantitative reasoning. Adaptive thinking scales analysis depth with problem complexity.

education.notes: Excellent tutoring with patient explanations. Effort parameter lets platforms balance quality against cost.

creative-writing.notes: Strong creative capabilities with nuanced character development and narrative flow.

strengths: Breakthrough abstract reasoning: 68.8% ARC-AGI-2 (up from Opus 4.5's 37.6%)
Best-in-class computer use at launch: 72.7% OSWorld
1M token context window (beta at launch) with 128K max output
Adaptive thinking replaces manual thinking budgets — no tuning required
Effort parameter GA including new 'max' level for compute control
Same $5/$25 pricing as Opus 4.5 despite major capability gains
limitations: Two generations behind current Opus 4.8 (still served, but no longer frontier)
Removed last-assistant-turn prefills — code relying on prefills returns 400
Higher latency than Sonnet models (~2.5s p50)
Premium pricing relative to Sonnet 4.6 ($5/$25 vs $3/$15)
best_for: Complex software engineering and long-horizon agentic coding
Abstract reasoning and novel problem solving (ARC-AGI-class tasks)
Enterprise workloads pinned to a stable, well-characterized Opus generation
not_recommended_for: New deployments where Opus 4.8 is available at the same price
Workflows still dependent on assistant-turn prefills

pricing.notes: Same pricing as Opus 4.5. Batch API 50% discount. Prompt caching up to 90% savings. Confirmed unchanged at $5/$25 as of 2026-07-09.

metadata.architecture: Transformer-based with Constitutional AI alignment, adaptive thinking, and effort parameter
